claim: Boundary snapshot implementation covers git ls-files tracked/cached/other files plus fallback walks and excludes dispatch artifact root, but has not been stress-tested with real Claude -p agents.

evidence.summary: gitFiles() uses git ls-files -co --exclude-standard -z to get cached, others, and exclude-standard files
evidence.why: Git ls-files -c covers cached/staged files, -o covers untracked/other files, and --exclude-standard respects .gitignore, providing comprehensive git-backed file listing.

evidence.summary: walkFiles() recursively walks the directory tree and excludes common build/cache dirs
evidence.why: Fallback walk coverage for non-git projects or to supplement git listing, excluding .git, node_modules, dist, coverage, .next, .cache.

evidence.summary: buildBoundarySnapshot() unions git and walked files, filters allowedRoots, and creates SHA-256 digests
evidence.why: Combines git ls-files output with walkFiles fallback using Set union, excludes dispatch artifact root via allowedRoots filter, and hashes file contents for mutation detection.

evidence.summary: diffBoundarySnapshots() compares before/after digests to detect added/modified/deleted mutations
evidence.why: Cryptographic SHA-256 digest comparison detects any file content change, addition, or deletion in the project boundary.

evidence.summary: Runtime builds boundary snapshots before/after agent execution and blocks on mutations
evidence.why: Lines 590 and 640 call buildBoundarySnapshot before and after agents, line 641 diffs them, and lines 642-646 report violations as BOUNDARY_PROJECT_MUTATION blockers.

evidence.summary: Test confirms git-backed boundary detects untracked project mutations
evidence.why: Creates untracked-mutation.txt during agent execution and verifies boundary.changes contains the added file and readiness has BOUNDARY_PROJECT_MUTATION blocker.

evidence.summary: Test confirms git-backed boundary detects gitignored mutations outside excluded dirs
evidence.why: Creates .env file (gitignored but not in excludedDirs list) and verifies boundary detects it as a mutation.

evidence.summary: Test confirms non-git boundary fallback detects modified files and ignores artifacts
evidence.why: In a non-git temporary directory, verifies modifying src/existing.txt is detected while writing to agent output directory (allowed artifact root) is ignored.

assumptions: git ls-files -co returns all tracked, cached, and untracked files while respecting --exclude-standard
walkFiles() fallback covers all files in non-git projects that are not in excludedDirs
Files in .git directory are correctly excluded by excludedDir check and do not need mutation detection
risks: Git ls-files may miss files that are both gitignored and in excludedDirs but still project-relevant
walkFiles union with gitFiles may include files that git ignores but the project does not care about
Dirty working tree with 55 modified files suggests the implementation itself has not been stress-tested against real agents
missing: Evidence from stress-testing with real Claude -p agents in actual projects
Evidence that boundary detection correctly handles symlinked files or special file types
Evidence that excludedDirs list is comprehensive for common project structures

result.notes: Boundary snapshot implementation correctly covers git ls-files plus walk fallback and excludes artifact root. All unit tests pass. The gap is operational: the dispatch system exists in a dirty working tree and has not been validated against real Claude agents with full tools. The claim is technically accurate but the operational risk remains unverified.
